item.title: Best smartwatches in 2024
item.itunes:title: Best smartwatches in 2024
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
A smartwatch is an extension of your smartphone. You get all the benefits of the best fitness trackers as well as access to your personal life, such as text messages, emails, calendars, and notifications. But when it comes to the best smartwatches, the choices are nothing short of abundant. Apple alone has several generations, all of which you can score discounts through ever-changing Apple watch deals.

But don’t let that abundance of choice scare you. This article has a great selection of the best smartwatches that will fit your needs. Even if you aren’t team Apple or Samsung, there are plenty of other options from top-tier tech brands like Garmin, Google, and Amazfit to meet your smartwatch needs.

  • Source: Samsung
    Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic
    Best smartwatch for Android users
    $340 at Samsung
  • Source: Apple
    Apple Watch Series 9
    Best for Apple users
    $329 at Amazon
  • Source: Google

    Great safety features

    Best for battery life

    Great for health enthusiasts

  • OnePlus Watch 2
    Best Wear OS watch for battery life

  • Source: Withings
    Withings ScanWatch 2
    Best hybrid smartwatch
    $350 at Best Buy
  • Source: Amazfit
    Amazfit T-Rex 2
    Best rugged smartwatch for most users

  • Source: Apple

    Premium Apple Watch

    Garmin Venu 3
    Best health-data insights
    $424 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazfit

    Best budget smartwatch
    $120 at Amazon

    Best budget smartwatch for iPhone users

    Garmin Bounce
    Best for kids
    $150 at Garmin

Our favorite smartwatches in 2024

Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic
Best smartwatch for Android users

A smartwatch packed with great heart sensor capabilities and great battery life.

The Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic can take over 90 different activities and make sure your heart is in check with heartbeat and rhythm notifications.

Pros
  • Automatic activity tracking
  • Good battery life
  • Bright display
Cons
  • Wristband is uncomfortable
  • Some health features require Samsung phone pairing
$394 at Amazon$340 at Samsung$400 at Best Buy

The watchmakers at Samsung listened to your feedback to bring what you liked from the Samsung Galaxy Watch 4 while advancing the tech from the Samsung Galaxy Watch 5. Making a comeback is the rotating bezel that XDA's former Editor, Chris Wedel, raved over in his review of the Galaxy Watch 6 Classic. Not only does the rotating bezel make for a simpler user experience, as it conveniently navigates through menus and scrolls through messages, but it also provides that oh-so-sweet click as you turn it.

Related
Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic review: An excellent generational mashup
The Galaxy Watch 6 Classic brings back a smartwatch favorite along with a spec bump. It's a great experience, even if it's too familiar

Aside from the bezel, the Watch 6 Classic sports a Super AMOLED display with Sapphire Crystal glass for added protection from scratches and scrapes. Throw in a brighter 2,000-nit display from previous versions, and not only is this one of the best-looking displays in the Samsung Smartwatch family, but it is also the brightest, helping you see the display even in direct sunlight. The battery found notable improvements as well from previous versions. You can expect about 40 hours of battery life, which smashes the likes of the Apple Series 8 Watch, which is only rated for 16 hours.

Another big advancement from previous versions is the ability to detect heart rhythm irregularities. The advanced sensors inside of the Watch 6 Classic will send you notifications if it detects an irregular heartbeat, helping you stay safe and healthy. Of course, all of the usual suspects are available as well, including heart rate, step counter, workout duration, and distance. But the Samsung Smartwatch can even recognize when you’ve begun certain activities, like swimming, even if you forgot to manually track it. This helps you leave no workout unaccounted for. If you’re looking for a new Android Watch, The Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic is a stellar choice.

Apple Watch Series 9
Best for Apple users

The best Apple Watch for most users

$329 $399 Save $70

The design of the Apple Watch stayed the same from previous iterations, but the features improved. The Double Tap gesture provides great functionality without needing to touch the display. And a new processor and brighter display makes the watch faster and easier to view in bad lighting conditions.

Pros
  • On-device Siri
  • Double Tap gesture
  • Bright display
Cons
  • Might not be worth upgrading if you have Series 7 or Series 8 Apple Watch
$329 at Amazon$349 at Best Buy

The “squircle” design remains the same compared to previous generations, but the 2,000-nit display helps you see the watch face better in harsh lighting conditions, such as direct sunlight. Along with a brighter display comes a new S9 processor. With the faster, more improved processor comes along on-device Siri. With it, you can ask Siri to do tasks like set an alarm, or create a reminder, without the need for an internet connection. It makes managing your watch, and your daily tasks, much easier.

Related
Apple Watch Series 9 review: More of the same, but it'll be enough
The Apple Watch Series 9 doesn't look that exciting, but the upgrades it packs make it one of the best smartwatches — again.

Much like the design, the battery life stayed relatively the same as well. You can expect about a full day's worth (24 hours) of battery life before it needs a recharge. Even though the design aesthetically looks the same, Apple is making huge strides environmentally by going completely carbon-neutral with this watch, crafting the watch itself, as well as its packaging, all from recycled materials.

Activity tracking remains elite. Although not new, Apple Watch Series 9 can detect when you’ve begun exercising, and track it, even if you forget to do it manually. It also has an accurate step counter and all the great heart rate monitoring and irregular heartbeat rhythm notification functionality that we’ve become accustomed to over past generations of Apple Watch.

Google Pixel Watch 2
Great safety features

The redo of the Pixel Watch is a winner

The Pixel Watch 2 makes the necessary improvements from the first generation. Improved all-day battery life and health tracking sensors help boost the overall quality of the watch. On-board health coaches can help you keep a specific pace while running, and also guide you through meditation sessions. It’s a great watch to manage your day- to-day.

Pros
  • All-day battery life
  • Improved health tracking
  • Great safety features
Cons
  • Only available in one size
$350 at Amazon$350 at Best Buy$350 at Google Store

Making improvements where it sorely needed it, the Pixel Watch 2 is a much better representation of a smartwatch that actually belongs in the conversation with the best smartwatches available. The battery life lasts all day, even with the great-looking 1.2-inch AMOLED display set to always-on.

Related
Google Pixel Watch 2 review: Second time's the charm
The Google Pixel Watch 2 fixes the glaring flaws of the first watch and keeps what worked. The price is still perhaps too high, though

One of the strengths of the Pixel Watch 2 is the synergy between smartwatch and phone. You can receive and respond to texts from the watch. It is compatible with WhatsApp, arguably the most widely used messaging app outside the U.S. And unsurprisingly, yet super helpful, apps like Gmail and Google Calendar also work as if you were on your phone or computer. All the more reason to keep your phone in your pocket.

A new heart-rate sensor is highly accurate, and the step counter does a great job to accurately count steps, and ignore fake one’s, like when you’re moving your arms around while you’re talking. A new electrodermal activity sensor also detects changes in your temperature to detect elevated levels of stress. When this happens you’ll receive notifications on how to reduce stress with suggested breathing and mindfulness exercises.

Garmin Instinct 2 Solar
Best for battery life

Military-grade protection and solar charging

The Garmin Instinct 2 Solar is perfect for those who spend most of their time outdoors. The smartwatch has an impressive 28-day max battery life and solar charging capabilities for less downtime. It also tracks a plethora of outdoor activities, everything from mountain to sea.

Pros
  • Incredible battery life solar charging
  • Rugged form-factor
  • Comes in two different sizes
Cons
  • Expensive price tag
  • No storage for music
$400 at Garmin$410 at Amazon$400 at Best Buy

The Garmin Instinct 2 Solar comes with the promise of "never-ending battery life" for active users who spend some time outdoors every day. This particular watch charges using a transparent "power-glass" covering the screen and the panels on the surrounding edges. This glass is capable of keeping the watch topped up for essentially unlimited battery life, provided you spend at least three hours every day in direct sunlight. The goal is to expose the watch to 50,000 lux of light every day, so you don't have to plug it into a charger for more juice.

Related
Best fitness trackers in 2024
From simple trackers to more sophisticated options that double up as a smartwatch, these are the best fitness trackers to buy in 2023.

The Garmin Instinct 2 Solar lacks a touchscreen, but it more than makes up for it with its intuitive button-based interface. It supports all the smart features that you'd expect from a typical smartwatch in 2022, including notification pop-ups, GPS, workout tracking, heart rate tracking, and more. The Instinct 2 Solar tracks a vast number of activities, including running, walking, cycling, swimming, strength training, cardio, and more. It syncs with the Garmin Connect app on your smartphone to give you additional insights too.

The Garmin Instinct 2 Solar comes in a bunch of different models, but the standard version with solar charging starts at $450. This particular smartwatch is not for everyone, and certainly not for those who spend a lot of time indoors. But if you're an active outdoor person who spends long enough each day in the sun, then you may never have to plug it in for charging.

Fitbit Sense 2
Great for health enthusiasts

A fitness tracker at heart, but still a decent smartwatch.

$247 $300 Save $53

With a slim profile and impressive battery life that can last up to six days, the Fitbit Sense 2 is a great companion for exercising and as an everyday wearable. It features automatic workout detection to help you keep track of your workouts and is available in several colors.

Pros
  • Lightweight
  • Great battery life
  • Google Maps and Google Wallet available
Cons
  • No music controls
$247 at Amazon$250 at Best Buy

The Fitbit Sense 2 has a lightweight, smooth and easy to wear profile that is built for all day comfort. That’s great considering there's a good chance you won’t need to take it off to charge for several days. The maximum achievable battery life is six days, but our Editor, Chris Wedel mostly experienced three days of battery life in his review of Sense 2. You can also get a full day’s worth of battery life just by charging your watch for 15 minutes. Long battery life and quick charging are both impressive and handy.

Related
Fitbit Sense 2 review: A fantastic wearable that straddles the line between two worlds
The Fitbit Sense 2 is walking a very fine line between being a smartwatch and a fitness tracker — great at one but not so much at the other.

It’s a bit odd that Google owns Fitbit, but Google Assistant isn’t available. Instead, you have access to Amazon Alexa on the watch for your hands-free needs. And when it comes to fitness data, it’s robust to say the least, but some of it is behind a subscription. For instance, if you want sleep data, such as Sleep Profiles, which provides insights on your sleep patterns, you’ll have to pay for Fitbit Premium which is $10 a month or discounted at $80 for the year. All of your health data automatically syncs with the Fitbit app, where you can go in-depth on your health and fitness tracking data.

And if you enjoy sharing your fitness accomplishments with a community of like-minded fellow exercisers, Fitbit has done a great job of creating a community to do so. You can share your fitness progress, and even compete with fellow enthusiasts through the smartwatch and Fitbit app.

OnePlus Watch 2
Best Wear OS watch for battery life

Multiday battery on a single charge

The OnePlus Watch 2 features a 1.43-inch OLED display and runs on Google's WearOS 4 for native support of Google services. It's also powered by two chips, including Qualcomm's Snapdragon W5, to handle energy efficiency and performance, giving it superior battery life to rival wearables from Google and Samsung. 

Pros
  • You don't need to bring a charger for a weekend trip
  • Great design and build quality
  • Reliable and snappy performance
Cons
  • On-screen keyboard difficult to type, and no option to scribble text
  • The crown is loose and can be twisted, but doesn't actually do anything
$300 at OnePlus

The OnePlus Watch 2 is the latest Wear OS smartwatch to land on the market, but this one is slightly different from the others that are out there. That's because the OnePlus Watch 2 promises to last for up to 100 hours on a single charge, which is a pretty big claim for a Wear OS smartwatch. My colleague Ben was able to able to test the battery life of this watch, and he says you can use it for up to four days with controlled, light usage. That makes it one of the best Wear OS watches out there for battery life.

Related
OnePlus Watch 2 review: The rare WearOS watch with good battery life
The OnePlus Watch 2 fixes the biggest issue of the first watch — by running WearOS, it finally has access to Google services that most rely on

The TicWatch Pro 5 that we reviewed last year offers similar battery power, but it also costs slightly more than the OnePlus Watch 2. The OnePlus Watch 2 can also achieve those battery life figures without relying on the secondary low-power display. It instead employs a relatively low-powered chipset called BES 2700 for basic tasks like tracking steps and keeping alarms. More intense and resource-intensive Wear-OS related tasks are handled by the Snapdragon W5 chip, which is known to be pretty reliable for everyday use.

As far as the other features are concerned, the OnePlus Watch 2 sports a 1.43-inch OLED screen with a resolution of 466x466, and up to 1,000 nits brightness. It also ships with Wear OS 4 out of the box, so it comes with all the bells and whistles you'd expect to see on a modern Wear OS watch. The OnePlus Watch 2 also has a heart rate monitor, and it also comes with features like workout detection and other fitness-centric features.

Withings ScanWatch 2
Best hybrid smartwatch

Offers the best of both worlds

The Withings ScanWatch 2 is an elegant looking classic styled watch which offers a great deal of fitness tracking capabilities. The timeless design along with excellent battery life make this a great choice for everyday wear.

Pros
  • Amazing 30 day battery life
  • Beautiful design
  • 24/7 temperature tracking
Cons
  • The display is small
$368 at Amazon$350 at Best Buy

Blending both analog and new smartwatch tech into a timeless looking design, the Withings ScanWatch 2 is as elegant looking as it is useful when it comes to health tracking. The ScanWatch 2 is available in two different finishes, silver and rose gold, as well as two different case sizes, 38mm and 42mm. The watch face features analog time hands with a small digital screen built within that displays all of your health data insights.

Like many other flagship smartwatches, the ScanWatch 2 offers heart health tracking and stats. This includes your average heart rate throughout the day, notifications for when your heart rate is elevated or too slow, and even tracking for irregular heart rhythms such as atrial fibrillation. A new addition to the ScanWatch 2 is 24/7 temperature tracking which can help you identify periods of stress, and even help you get better sleep. Additional insights include cycle tracking for women.

If you have the first generation of the Withings ScanWatch, there might not be enough difference between the two to justify upgrading. If you don’t, and you’re looking for the best of the analog and digital worlds, the Withings ScanWatch 2 is a solid choice.

Amazfit T-Rex 2
Best rugged smartwatch for most users

The one that works with both Android and iOS

This military-tested smartwatch can handle extreme circumstances like extreme heat, cold and weather, yet is completely environmentally conscious with biodegradable components. It also boasts an impressive 150 built-in sports modes and can last up to 24 days without a charge.

Pros
  • Durable build quality
  • Long battery life
  • Support for various fitness activities
Cons
  • Zepp app needs more work
  • Bulky form-factor
$200 at Amazon

The Amazfit T-Rex 2 is one of those smartwatches that's very easy for us to recommend. It costs less than $200, has a rugged look with impressive build quality to match, and it lasts for over two weeks on a single charge. It may not be the most premium or the most feature-packed smartwatch out there on the market, but it works as advertised, and it excels at plenty of things that'll make it a worthwhile purchase.

Related
Amazfit T-Rex 2 Review: Heavy-duty fitness tracking
The Amazfit T-Rex 2 packs a lot of great features and it also promises to offer a long battery life. Is it worth buying? Let's find out.

One of the best things about the Amazfit T-Rex 2 is its build quality. It's got a rugged build with 10ATM water resistance and STD-MIL-810G certification. It's not designed to everyone's liking, but it speaks well with those who are into buying rugged smartwatches to go with their active lifestyle. As we mentioned in our T-Rex 2 review, it looks like a G-Shock watch, so it's definitely not for those who like minimal-looking sports watches.

The Amazfit T-Rex 2 is only available in one size, though. It's both bigger and bulkier than many other smartwatches on the market, but thankfully it's not as heavy as it looks. The T-Rex 2 is also validated to withstand extreme temperatures and environmental conditions from 70 degrees Celsius to all the way down to -40 degrees Celsius. It also sports an AMOLED panel on the front which we found to be just as bright and beautiful as the displays on some other premium smartwatches on the market.

The Amazfit T-Rex 2 runs on the company's custom software, but it offers a good set of features to complement the hardware. From heart-rate monitoring to automatic sleep tracking, the Amazfit T-Rex 2 can do it all, making it a lot better than a lot of other smartwatches out there. Some other noteworthy features of this watch include the ability to measure blood oxygen (SpO2) and stress levels, support for GPS, and a lot more. If you're looking for the most rugged, feature-packed option from Amazfit, then check out the T-Rex Ultra. It ups the ante on everything the T-Rex 2 offers, not just in hardware and features but also in price.

Apple Watch Ultra 2
Premium Apple Watch

Apple’s most advanced and rugged smartwatch

$738 $799 Save $61

The Apple Watch Ultra 2 is a durable and advanced smartwatch equipped with the new S9 processor. Boasting new features like the Double Tap gesture, you can control certain functions without ever actually touching your watch. The display is also big, bright, and looks great.

Pros
  • New Double Tap gesture is convenient
  • S9 chip is fast
  • Display is bright
Cons
  • Battery life stayed the same from previous generation
$738 at Amazon$799 at Best Buy

As far as major advancements from the first Apple Watch Ultra to the Ultra 2, there’s nothing earth-shattering. But the new 3,000 nit display is great. The brighter screen helps you see through harsh lighting conditions, such as direct sunlight. In the Apple Watch Ultra 2, you also get a newer, faster S9 processor which not only helps improve processing speeds but helps make Siri more accurate.

Related
Apple Watch Ultra 2 review: It's just a giant Apple Watch
The most Ultra watch to date.

The big advancement is the Double Tap gesture. Simply by tapping your index finger and thumb together twice, you can control certain aspects of your watch without ever having to actually touch the watchface. For instance, if you’re walking with a coffee in one hand and someone calls, you can quickly answer the call by double-tapping the fingers on your watch hand. Or, if you’re trying to take a group photo, the Double Tap gesture can work as a shutter while you’re away from your phone. The Double Tap feature has a handful of additional functions as well.

A feature called Precision Finding helps you find your phone when it’s misplaced. When you’re searching for your phone, the watch face can display the distance and point you in the right direction of your phone. And as you get closer, your watch and phone will vibrate and make noise to help you finally locate the device. Of course, this only works with the latest iPhone 15.

Garmin Venu 3
Best health-data insights

A smartwatch with strong focus on your overall well being.

$424 $450 Save $26

The battery can last anywhere between five and 20 days depending on how you use it. Even on the short end, that is still exponentially better than the majority of smartwatches. The health and recovery data is robust to help you be the most fit, and healthiest, both physically and mentally, version of yourself.

Pros
  • Incredible battery life
  • Great recovery insights
  • Available in two sizes
Cons
  • Expensive
$424 at Amazon$450 at Garmin

The Garmin Venu 3 is available in both a 41mm and 45mm model. Regardless of your preference, you still get the benefit of amazing battery life. If charging your devices is a bane for you, you’ll appreciate the five to 20-day battery life, depending on your use. Even at five days, it’s still several days better than the likes of both Apple Watches on this list.

The health and fitness data are as robust as the battery life. It comes with 30 preloaded sports. Garmin also included exercises for those wheelchair-bound, which is a great inclusionary feature. And when you’re done exercising, the Venu 3 offers a plethora of recovery data such as your sleep score, as well as things you can do to improve your sleep through an “Evening Report.” The watch also offers guided meditation with health data tracking, so you can actually see how your body is reacting to the meditation.

The Garmin Venu 3 provides both Android and Apple call support. It can even connect to Siri. One of the only real blemishes is its lack of LTE support. But it's clear that the smartwatch is far more focused on health and recovery, as opposed to keeping your phone in your pocket.

Amazfit GTR Mini
Best budget smartwatch

Compact, affordable, and comfortable to wear

Amazfit's GTR Mini brings a classically styled watch that looks great and is comfortable to wear and a fully packed fitness companion.

Pros
  • Very lightweight and comfortable to wear
  • Excellent battery life
Cons
  • Very limited app support
  • Can only use preset responses for notifications
$120 at Amazon$120 at Amazfit

The Amazfit GTR Mini has now replaced the Fitbit Versa 3 as our pick for the best budget smartwatch you can buy in 2024. You can buy this smartwatch from Amazon and Amazfit's website for as little as $120. That's a pretty good price for a watch that's packed with some awesome features. As mentioned in our Amazfit GTR Mini review, it goes head-to-head with a bunch of other premium watches with its fitness tracking features. It can track over 120 different workouts and exercises, including walking, running, biking, and more. You also get all the basic options to track things like water intake, calories, and more.

Related
Amazfit GTR Mini review: Big battery life and excellent fitness tracking in a small package
The Amazfit GTR Mini is a battery beast in a slim package with loads of health and fitness monitoring for a great price.

The Amazfit GTR Mini comes with a BiotTracker 3.0 sensor that offers real-time, 24-hour monitoring of your heart rate, blood oxygen levels, and stress, among other things. You also get support for GPS, and it can withstand the equivalent of up to 50 meters of water pressure. Amazfit is using its in-house ZeppOS 2.0 to power this watch, and it comes with a lot of good features. You don't get support for a large selection of third-party apps on this watch, though, so keep that in mind.

The Amazfit GTR Mini is a pretty-looking watch that is wrapped in stainless steel and plastic, and it only weighs 36.2g. It sports a 1.28-inch AMOLED panel, and it packs a 280mAh battery that can go as long as 30 days before needing a charge. There's plenty to like about the Amazfit GTR Mini, and it's easily one of the best budget watches on the market right now.

Apple Watch SE 2
Best budget smartwatch for iPhone users

Experience the best Apple Watch features for less

A smart choice for Apple tech users looking to save a little coin. The Apple Watch SE 2 is lighter on features, forgoing features such as the always-on display, and ECG found in other Apple watches. But if you don't mind missing out on a few items, this is a great way to get an Apple Watch on your wrist at a discounted price.

Pros
  • Upgraded internals
  • Relatively affordable price tag
Cons
  • Slightly thicker bezels
  • No always on display
$240 at Amazon$249 at Best Buy$249 at Apple

In addition to the new Apple Watch Series 8 and the Apple Watch Ultra, the Cupertino tech giant also introduced the new Apple Watch SE at its Far Out event last year. The Apple Watch SE (2022) replaces the original Apple Watch SE with an updated chip, a slightly redesigned casing, and more. It also starts at a lower price now compared to the original Apple Watch SE (2020), making it a solid option for those who are looking to buy a cheaper Apple Watch to pair with their new iPhones.

Related
Apple Watch Series 9 vs Watch SE 2: Which smartwatch should you get?
Should you pick up an all-new Series 9, or should you save some cash and grab an SE 2?

Apple didn't launch a new SE Watch model alongside the Apple Watch Series 9, but the regular Apple Watch SE from 2022 is still worth considering if you are already stretching your budget trying to buy a watch to go with your iPhone. It debuted along the Apple Watch Series 8 model and the Apple Watch Ultra, and it packs the S8 chip which is still pretty reliable. As far as the features are concerned, the Apple Watch SE (2022) supports heart rate monitoring and the ability to track all the standard sets of workouts. However, you'll be missing out on ECG, SpO2, and even the new temperature sensor, all of which are supported by the mainline Apple Watch models.

In terms of design, the new Apple Watch SE (2022) looks very similar to a lot of other modern Apple Watches on the market. It has thicker bezels compared to the new Series 9, but you can mask it rather easily by using a darker watch face. The bottom case of the Apple Watch SE (2022) is made of nylon composite materials that are color-matched to the bezels. The overall performance of the Apple Watch SE should be significantly better than its predecessor, and it works very well for the most part. The new Apple Watch SE has also been updated to the latest watchOS version, and you won't miss out on any essentials like the crash detection feature.

Garmin Bounce
Best for kids

A reliable watch with text and voice messaging

The Garmin Bounce introduces kids to health insights and gamifies moving around to help promote healthy habits. Parents can also locate their kids through the watch and reach them via text which kids and read and respond right on the screen.

Pros
  • Supports two-way text and voice messaging
  • Safety controls including location tracking and emergency contact support
  • Parental controls with contact list management support
Cons
  • Requires LTE subscription exclusive to Garmin
  • Does not support phone calls
$150 at Garmin

If you're not ready to buy a full-fledged smartwatch for your kids yet, then consider checking out the Garmin Bounce. It's a kid-friendly device offering good connectivity, parental controls, and a host of other essentials. It also comes with a bunch of preloaded apps and games that your kids will find useful for everyday use. Additionally, this particular watch will also reward your kids with more features and fun activities in the Garmin Jr. app as they move and clock more activity in the real world while wearing the watch.

Related
Garmin Bounce kids smartwatch review: Will make both kids and parents happy
The Garmin Bounce isn't a flashy smartwatch for kids, but it performs as advertised, making is great option for families.

The only thing to note about this particular smartwatch is that you'll need to subscribe to a proprietary Garmin subscription plan to unlock its LTE connectivity. It's essential to access features like text and voice messages, so keep that in mind. Parents can also take advantage of the parental control features to access their ward's real-time location with notifications and enable the class mode to ensure they're not distracted by the watch during schoolwork.

The Garmin Bounce is a simple-looking watch that's lightweight and feels comfortable to wear every day. It sports a 1.3-inch LCD touchscreen and a handful of buttons on the sides to help you navigate through the watch. It also offers excellent battery life, so you don't have to worry about charging it every day or every time your kid is about to step out of the house.

There's no shortage of reliable smartwatches on the market in 2024

Out of all the options mentioned in this particular collection, the Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic and the Apple Watch Series 9 are two of the best smartwatches you can buy right now. The Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic has a beautiful circular design that resembles a timeless analog timepiece, but with all the advanced tech you’d expect in a flagship smartwatch, such as ECG, 90-plus activities and a 2,000 nit bright display. The Apple Series 9 also has a bright 2,000 nit display, and many of the same features you’ll find on the Ultra 2, including the blazing fast S9 chip, but for a smaller price tag.

The Galaxy Watch 6 Classic beautifully blends the Watch 4 and the Watch 5, bringing back the physical turning bezel that makes navigating the UI a breeze. The aforementioned battery life is an improvement from previous generations as well as the Exynos W930 processor, which makes navigating through your watch a consistently smooth endeavor.

  • Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic
    Best smartwatch for Android users

    The Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic can take over 90 different activities and make sure your heart is in check with heartbeat and rhythm notifications.

    $394 at Amazon$340 at Samsung
  • Apple Watch Series 9
    Best smartwatch for Apple users
    $329 $399 Save $70

    Powered by the blazing fast S9 chip, you not only get a faster and brighter watch, but also Siri that doesn't require internet connection.

    $329 at Amazon$349 at Best Buy$399 at Apple

The Apple Watch Series 9, on the other hand, is a solid option for iPhone users. It's not as kitted as the Ultra model, but the Series 9 has the same S9 chip, and the ever-so-useful Double Tap gesture feature for hands-free control of your watch and phone. On-board Siri is also a boon and makes it easy to manage tasks like timers and reminders without the need for internet connection.

This is an ever-evolving list, so we'll continue to keep an eye on the market to see if we can add new options to this post.
item.description:
A smartwatch is an extension of your smartphone. You get all the benefits of the best fitness trackers as well as access to your personal life, such as text messages, emails, calendars, and notifications. But when it comes to the best smartwatches, the choices are nothing short of abundant. Apple alone has several generations, all of which you can score discounts through ever-changing Apple watch deals.

But don’t let that abundance of choice scare you. This article has a great selection of the best smartwatches that will fit your needs. Even if you aren’t team Apple or Samsung, there are plenty of other options from top-tier tech brands like Garmin, Google, and Amazfit to meet your smartwatch needs.

  • Source: Samsung
    Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic
    Best smartwatch for Android users
    $340 at Samsung
  • Source: Apple
    Apple Watch Series 9
    Best for Apple users
    $329 at Amazon
  • Source: Google
    Google Pixel Watch 2
    Great safety features
    $350 at Amazon
  • Source: Garmin
    Garmin Instinct 2 Solar
    Best for battery life
    $400 at Garmin
  • Fitbit Sense 2
    Great for health enthusiasts
    $247 at Amazon
  • OnePlus Watch 2
    Best Wear OS watch for battery life
    $300 at OnePlus
  • Source: Withings
    Withings ScanWatch 2
    Best hybrid smartwatch
    $350 at Best Buy
  • Source: Amazfit
    Amazfit T-Rex 2
    Best rugged smartwatch for most users
    $200 at Amazon
  • Source: Apple
    Apple Watch Ultra 2
    Premium Apple Watch
    $738 at Amazon
  • Source: Garmin
    Garmin Venu 3
    Best health-data insights
    $424 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazfit
    Amazfit GTR Mini
    Best budget smartwatch
    $120 at Amazon
  • Source: Apple
    Apple Watch SE 2
    Best budget smartwatch for iPhone users
    $240 at Amazon
  • Source: Garmin
    Garmin Bounce
    Best for kids
    $150 at Garmin

Our favorite smartwatches in 2024

Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic
Best smartwatch for Android users

A smartwatch packed with great heart sensor capabilities and great battery life.

The Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic can take over 90 different activities and make sure your heart is in check with heartbeat and rhythm notifications.

Pros
  • Automatic activity tracking
  • Good battery life
  • Bright display
Cons
  • Wristband is uncomfortable
  • Some health features require Samsung phone pairing
$394 at Amazon$340 at Samsung$400 at Best Buy

The watchmakers at Samsung listened to your feedback to bring what you liked from the Samsung Galaxy Watch 4 while advancing the tech from the Samsung Galaxy Watch 5. Making a comeback is the rotating bezel that XDA's former Editor, Chris Wedel, raved over in his review of the Galaxy Watch 6 Classic. Not only does the rotating bezel make for a simpler user experience, as it conveniently navigates through menus and scrolls through messages, but it also provides that oh-so-sweet click as you turn it.

Related
Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic review: An excellent generational mashup
The Galaxy Watch 6 Classic brings back a smartwatch favorite along with a spec bump. It's a great experience, even if it's too familiar

Aside from the bezel, the Watch 6 Classic sports a Super AMOLED display with Sapphire Crystal glass for added protection from scratches and scrapes. Throw in a brighter 2,000-nit display from previous versions, and not only is this one of the best-looking displays in the Samsung Smartwatch family, but it is also the brightest, helping you see the display even in direct sunlight. The battery found notable improvements as well from previous versions. You can expect about 40 hours of battery life, which smashes the likes of the Apple Series 8 Watch, which is only rated for 16 hours.

Another big advancement from previous versions is the ability to detect heart rhythm irregularities. The advanced sensors inside of the Watch 6 Classic will send you notifications if it detects an irregular heartbeat, helping you stay safe and healthy. Of course, all of the usual suspects are available as well, including heart rate, step counter, workout duration, and distance. But the Samsung Smartwatch can even recognize when you’ve begun certain activities, like swimming, even if you forgot to manually track it. This helps you leave no workout unaccounted for. If you’re looking for a new Android Watch, The Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic is a stellar choice.

Apple Watch Series 9
Best for Apple users

The best Apple Watch for most users

$329 $399 Save $70

The design of the Apple Watch stayed the same from previous iterations, but the features improved. The Double Tap gesture provides great functionality without needing to touch the display. And a new processor and brighter display makes the watch faster and easier to view in bad lighting conditions.

Pros
  • On-device Siri
  • Double Tap gesture
  • Bright display
Cons
  • Might not be worth upgrading if you have Series 7 or Series 8 Apple Watch
$329 at Amazon$349 at Best Buy

The “squircle” design remains the same compared to previous generations, but the 2,000-nit display helps you see the watch face better in harsh lighting conditions, such as direct sunlight. Along with a brighter display comes a new S9 processor. With the faster, more improved processor comes along on-device Siri. With it, you can ask Siri to do tasks like set an alarm, or create a reminder, without the need for an internet connection. It makes managing your watch, and your daily tasks, much easier.

Related
Apple Watch Series 9 review: More of the same, but it'll be enough
The Apple Watch Series 9 doesn't look that exciting, but the upgrades it packs make it one of the best smartwatches — again.

Much like the design, the battery life stayed relatively the same as well. You can expect about a full day's worth (24 hours) of battery life before it needs a recharge. Even though the design aesthetically looks the same, Apple is making huge strides environmentally by going completely carbon-neutral with this watch, crafting the watch itself, as well as its packaging, all from recycled materials.

Activity tracking remains elite. Although not new, Apple Watch Series 9 can detect when you’ve begun exercising, and track it, even if you forget to do it manually. It also has an accurate step counter and all the great heart rate monitoring and irregular heartbeat rhythm notification functionality that we’ve become accustomed to over past generations of Apple Watch.

Google Pixel Watch 2
Great safety features

The redo of the Pixel Watch is a winner

The Pixel Watch 2 makes the necessary improvements from the first generation. Improved all-day battery life and health tracking sensors help boost the overall quality of the watch. On-board health coaches can help you keep a specific pace while running, and also guide you through meditation sessions. It’s a great watch to manage your day- to-day.

Pros
  • All-day battery life
  • Improved health tracking
  • Great safety features
Cons
  • Only available in one size
$350 at Amazon$350 at Best Buy$350 at Google Store

Making improvements where it sorely needed it, the Pixel Watch 2 is a much better representation of a smartwatch that actually belongs in the conversation with the best smartwatches available. The battery life lasts all day, even with the great-looking 1.2-inch AMOLED display set to always-on.

Related
Google Pixel Watch 2 review: Second time's the charm
The Google Pixel Watch 2 fixes the glaring flaws of the first watch and keeps what worked. The price is still perhaps too high, though

One of the strengths of the Pixel Watch 2 is the synergy between smartwatch and phone. You can receive and respond to texts from the watch. It is compatible with WhatsApp, arguably the most widely used messaging app outside the U.S. And unsurprisingly, yet super helpful, apps like Gmail and Google Calendar also work as if you were on your phone or computer. All the more reason to keep your phone in your pocket.

A new heart-rate sensor is highly accurate, and the step counter does a great job to accurately count steps, and ignore fake one’s, like when you’re moving your arms around while you’re talking. A new electrodermal activity sensor also detects changes in your temperature to detect elevated levels of stress. When this happens you’ll receive notifications on how to reduce stress with suggested breathing and mindfulness exercises.

Garmin Instinct 2 Solar
Best for battery life

Military-grade protection and solar charging

The Garmin Instinct 2 Solar is perfect for those who spend most of their time outdoors. The smartwatch has an impressive 28-day max battery life and solar charging capabilities for less downtime. It also tracks a plethora of outdoor activities, everything from mountain to sea.

Pros
  • Incredible battery life solar charging
  • Rugged form-factor
  • Comes in two different sizes
Cons
  • Expensive price tag
  • No storage for music
$400 at Garmin$410 at Amazon$400 at Best Buy

The Garmin Instinct 2 Solar comes with the promise of "never-ending battery life" for active users who spend some time outdoors every day. This particular watch charges using a transparent "power-glass" covering the screen and the panels on the surrounding edges. This glass is capable of keeping the watch topped up for essentially unlimited battery life, provided you spend at least three hours every day in direct sunlight. The goal is to expose the watch to 50,000 lux of light every day, so you don't have to plug it into a charger for more juice.

Related
Best fitness trackers in 2024
From simple trackers to more sophisticated options that double up as a smartwatch, these are the best fitness trackers to buy in 2023.

The Garmin Instinct 2 Solar lacks a touchscreen, but it more than makes up for it with its intuitive button-based interface. It supports all the smart features that you'd expect from a typical smartwatch in 2022, including notification pop-ups, GPS, workout tracking, heart rate tracking, and more. The Instinct 2 Solar tracks a vast number of activities, including running, walking, cycling, swimming, strength training, cardio, and more. It syncs with the Garmin Connect app on your smartphone to give you additional insights too.

The Garmin Instinct 2 Solar comes in a bunch of different models, but the standard version with solar charging starts at $450. This particular smartwatch is not for everyone, and certainly not for those who spend a lot of time indoors. But if you're an active outdoor person who spends long enough each day in the sun, then you may never have to plug it in for charging.

Fitbit Sense 2
Great for health enthusiasts

A fitness tracker at heart, but still a decent smartwatch.

$247 $300 Save $53

With a slim profile and impressive battery life that can last up to six days, the Fitbit Sense 2 is a great companion for exercising and as an everyday wearable. It features automatic workout detection to help you keep track of your workouts and is available in several colors.

Pros
  • Lightweight
  • Great battery life
  • Google Maps and Google Wallet available
Cons
  • No music controls
$247 at Amazon$250 at Best Buy

The Fitbit Sense 2 has a lightweight, smooth and easy to wear profile that is built for all day comfort. That’s great considering there's a good chance you won’t need to take it off to charge for several days. The maximum achievable battery life is six days, but our Editor, Chris Wedel mostly experienced three days of battery life in his review of Sense 2. You can also get a full day’s worth of battery life just by charging your watch for 15 minutes. Long battery life and quick charging are both impressive and handy.

Related
Fitbit Sense 2 review: A fantastic wearable that straddles the line between two worlds
The Fitbit Sense 2 is walking a very fine line between being a smartwatch and a fitness tracker — great at one but not so much at the other.

It’s a bit odd that Google owns Fitbit, but Google Assistant isn’t available. Instead, you have access to Amazon Alexa on the watch for your hands-free needs. And when it comes to fitness data, it’s robust to say the least, but some of it is behind a subscription. For instance, if you want sleep data, such as Sleep Profiles, which provides insights on your sleep patterns, you’ll have to pay for Fitbit Premium which is $10 a month or discounted at $80 for the year. All of your health data automatically syncs with the Fitbit app, where you can go in-depth on your health and fitness tracking data.

And if you enjoy sharing your fitness accomplishments with a community of like-minded fellow exercisers, Fitbit has done a great job of creating a community to do so. You can share your fitness progress, and even compete with fellow enthusiasts through the smartwatch and Fitbit app.

OnePlus Watch 2
Best Wear OS watch for battery life

Multiday battery on a single charge

The OnePlus Watch 2 features a 1.43-inch OLED display and runs on Google's WearOS 4 for native support of Google services. It's also powered by two chips, including Qualcomm's Snapdragon W5, to handle energy efficiency and performance, giving it superior battery life to rival wearables from Google and Samsung. 

Pros
  • You don't need to bring a charger for a weekend trip
  • Great design and build quality
  • Reliable and snappy performance
Cons
  • On-screen keyboard difficult to type, and no option to scribble text
  • The crown is loose and can be twisted, but doesn't actually do anything
$300 at OnePlus

The OnePlus Watch 2 is the latest Wear OS smartwatch to land on the market, but this one is slightly different from the others that are out there. That's because the OnePlus Watch 2 promises to last for up to 100 hours on a single charge, which is a pretty big claim for a Wear OS smartwatch. My colleague Ben was able to able to test the battery life of this watch, and he says you can use it for up to four days with controlled, light usage. That makes it one of the best Wear OS watches out there for battery life.

Related
OnePlus Watch 2 review: The rare WearOS watch with good battery life
The OnePlus Watch 2 fixes the biggest issue of the first watch — by running WearOS, it finally has access to Google services that most rely on

The TicWatch Pro 5 that we reviewed last year offers similar battery power, but it also costs slightly more than the OnePlus Watch 2. The OnePlus Watch 2 can also achieve those battery life figures without relying on the secondary low-power display. It instead employs a relatively low-powered chipset called BES 2700 for basic tasks like tracking steps and keeping alarms. More intense and resource-intensive Wear-OS related tasks are handled by the Snapdragon W5 chip, which is known to be pretty reliable for everyday use.

As far as the other features are concerned, the OnePlus Watch 2 sports a 1.43-inch OLED screen with a resolution of 466x466, and up to 1,000 nits brightness. It also ships with Wear OS 4 out of the box, so it comes with all the bells and whistles you'd expect to see on a modern Wear OS watch. The OnePlus Watch 2 also has a heart rate monitor, and it also comes with features like workout detection and other fitness-centric features.

Withings ScanWatch 2
Best hybrid smartwatch

Offers the best of both worlds

The Withings ScanWatch 2 is an elegant looking classic styled watch which offers a great deal of fitness tracking capabilities. The timeless design along with excellent battery life make this a great choice for everyday wear.

Pros
  • Amazing 30 day battery life
  • Beautiful design
  • 24/7 temperature tracking
Cons
  • The display is small
$368 at Amazon$350 at Best Buy

Blending both analog and new smartwatch tech into a timeless looking design, the Withings ScanWatch 2 is as elegant looking as it is useful when it comes to health tracking. The ScanWatch 2 is available in two different finishes, silver and rose gold, as well as two different case sizes, 38mm and 42mm. The watch face features analog time hands with a small digital screen built within that displays all of your health data insights.

Like many other flagship smartwatches, the ScanWatch 2 offers heart health tracking and stats. This includes your average heart rate throughout the day, notifications for when your heart rate is elevated or too slow, and even tracking for irregular heart rhythms such as atrial fibrillation. A new addition to the ScanWatch 2 is 24/7 temperature tracking which can help you identify periods of stress, and even help you get better sleep. Additional insights include cycle tracking for women.

If you have the first generation of the Withings ScanWatch, there might not be enough difference between the two to justify upgrading. If you don’t, and you’re looking for the best of the analog and digital worlds, the Withings ScanWatch 2 is a solid choice.

Amazfit T-Rex 2
Best rugged smartwatch for most users

The one that works with both Android and iOS

This military-tested smartwatch can handle extreme circumstances like extreme heat, cold and weather, yet is completely environmentally conscious with biodegradable components. It also boasts an impressive 150 built-in sports modes and can last up to 24 days without a charge.

Pros
  • Durable build quality
  • Long battery life
  • Support for various fitness activities
Cons
  • Zepp app needs more work
  • Bulky form-factor
$200 at Amazon

The Amazfit T-Rex 2 is one of those smartwatches that's very easy for us to recommend. It costs less than $200, has a rugged look with impressive build quality to match, and it lasts for over two weeks on a single charge. It may not be the most premium or the most feature-packed smartwatch out there on the market, but it works as advertised, and it excels at plenty of things that'll make it a worthwhile purchase.

Related
Amazfit T-Rex 2 Review: Heavy-duty fitness tracking
The Amazfit T-Rex 2 packs a lot of great features and it also promises to offer a long battery life. Is it worth buying? Let's find out.

One of the best things about the Amazfit T-Rex 2 is its build quality. It's got a rugged build with 10ATM water resistance and STD-MIL-810G certification. It's not designed to everyone's liking, but it speaks well with those who are into buying rugged smartwatches to go with their active lifestyle. As we mentioned in our T-Rex 2 review, it looks like a G-Shock watch, so it's definitely not for those who like minimal-looking sports watches.

The Amazfit T-Rex 2 is only available in one size, though. It's both bigger and bulkier than many other smartwatches on the market, but thankfully it's not as heavy as it looks. The T-Rex 2 is also validated to withstand extreme temperatures and environmental conditions from 70 degrees Celsius to all the way down to -40 degrees Celsius. It also sports an AMOLED panel on the front which we found to be just as bright and beautiful as the displays on some other premium smartwatches on the market.

The Amazfit T-Rex 2 runs on the company's custom software, but it offers a good set of features to complement the hardware. From heart-rate monitoring to automatic sleep tracking, the Amazfit T-Rex 2 can do it all, making it a lot better than a lot of other smartwatches out there. Some other noteworthy features of this watch include the ability to measure blood oxygen (SpO2) and stress levels, support for GPS, and a lot more. If you're looking for the most rugged, feature-packed option from Amazfit, then check out the T-Rex Ultra. It ups the ante on everything the T-Rex 2 offers, not just in hardware and features but also in price.

Apple Watch Ultra 2
Premium Apple Watch

Apple’s most advanced and rugged smartwatch

$738 $799 Save $61

The Apple Watch Ultra 2 is a durable and advanced smartwatch equipped with the new S9 processor. Boasting new features like the Double Tap gesture, you can control certain functions without ever actually touching your watch. The display is also big, bright, and looks great.

Pros
  • New Double Tap gesture is convenient
  • S9 chip is fast
  • Display is bright
Cons
  • Battery life stayed the same from previous generation
$738 at Amazon$799 at Best Buy

As far as major advancements from the first Apple Watch Ultra to the Ultra 2, there’s nothing earth-shattering. But the new 3,000 nit display is great. The brighter screen helps you see through harsh lighting conditions, such as direct sunlight. In the Apple Watch Ultra 2, you also get a newer, faster S9 processor which not only helps improve processing speeds but helps make Siri more accurate.

Related
Apple Watch Ultra 2 review: It's just a giant Apple Watch
The most Ultra watch to date.

The big advancement is the Double Tap gesture. Simply by tapping your index finger and thumb together twice, you can control certain aspects of your watch without ever having to actually touch the watchface. For instance, if you’re walking with a coffee in one hand and someone calls, you can quickly answer the call by double-tapping the fingers on your watch hand. Or, if you’re trying to take a group photo, the Double Tap gesture can work as a shutter while you’re away from your phone. The Double Tap feature has a handful of additional functions as well.

A feature called Precision Finding helps you find your phone when it’s misplaced. When you’re searching for your phone, the watch face can display the distance and point you in the right direction of your phone. And as you get closer, your watch and phone will vibrate and make noise to help you finally locate the device. Of course, this only works with the latest iPhone 15.

Garmin Venu 3
Best health-data insights

A smartwatch with strong focus on your overall well being.

$424 $450 Save $26

The battery can last anywhere between five and 20 days depending on how you use it. Even on the short end, that is still exponentially better than the majority of smartwatches. The health and recovery data is robust to help you be the most fit, and healthiest, both physically and mentally, version of yourself.

Pros
  • Incredible battery life
  • Great recovery insights
  • Available in two sizes
Cons
  • Expensive
$424 at Amazon$450 at Garmin

The Garmin Venu 3 is available in both a 41mm and 45mm model. Regardless of your preference, you still get the benefit of amazing battery life. If charging your devices is a bane for you, you’ll appreciate the five to 20-day battery life, depending on your use. Even at five days, it’s still several days better than the likes of both Apple Watches on this list.

The health and fitness data are as robust as the battery life. It comes with 30 preloaded sports. Garmin also included exercises for those wheelchair-bound, which is a great inclusionary feature. And when you’re done exercising, the Venu 3 offers a plethora of recovery data such as your sleep score, as well as things you can do to improve your sleep through an “Evening Report.” The watch also offers guided meditation with health data tracking, so you can actually see how your body is reacting to the meditation.

The Garmin Venu 3 provides both Android and Apple call support. It can even connect to Siri. One of the only real blemishes is its lack of LTE support. But it's clear that the smartwatch is far more focused on health and recovery, as opposed to keeping your phone in your pocket.

Amazfit GTR Mini
Best budget smartwatch

Compact, affordable, and comfortable to wear

Amazfit's GTR Mini brings a classically styled watch that looks great and is comfortable to wear and a fully packed fitness companion.

Pros
  • Very lightweight and comfortable to wear
  • Excellent battery life
Cons
  • Very limited app support
  • Can only use preset responses for notifications
$120 at Amazon$120 at Amazfit

The Amazfit GTR Mini has now replaced the Fitbit Versa 3 as our pick for the best budget smartwatch you can buy in 2024. You can buy this smartwatch from Amazon and Amazfit's website for as little as $120. That's a pretty good price for a watch that's packed with some awesome features. As mentioned in our Amazfit GTR Mini review, it goes head-to-head with a bunch of other premium watches with its fitness tracking features. It can track over 120 different workouts and exercises, including walking, running, biking, and more. You also get all the basic options to track things like water intake, calories, and more.

Related
Amazfit GTR Mini review: Big battery life and excellent fitness tracking in a small package
The Amazfit GTR Mini is a battery beast in a slim package with loads of health and fitness monitoring for a great price.

The Amazfit GTR Mini comes with a BiotTracker 3.0 sensor that offers real-time, 24-hour monitoring of your heart rate, blood oxygen levels, and stress, among other things. You also get support for GPS, and it can withstand the equivalent of up to 50 meters of water pressure. Amazfit is using its in-house ZeppOS 2.0 to power this watch, and it comes with a lot of good features. You don't get support for a large selection of third-party apps on this watch, though, so keep that in mind.

The Amazfit GTR Mini is a pretty-looking watch that is wrapped in stainless steel and plastic, and it only weighs 36.2g. It sports a 1.28-inch AMOLED panel, and it packs a 280mAh battery that can go as long as 30 days before needing a charge. There's plenty to like about the Amazfit GTR Mini, and it's easily one of the best budget watches on the market right now.

Apple Watch SE 2
Best budget smartwatch for iPhone users

Experience the best Apple Watch features for less

A smart choice for Apple tech users looking to save a little coin. The Apple Watch SE 2 is lighter on features, forgoing features such as the always-on display, and ECG found in other Apple watches. But if you don't mind missing out on a few items, this is a great way to get an Apple Watch on your wrist at a discounted price.

Pros
  • Upgraded internals
  • Relatively affordable price tag
Cons
  • Slightly thicker bezels
  • No always on display
$240 at Amazon$249 at Best Buy$249 at Apple

In addition to the new Apple Watch Series 8 and the Apple Watch Ultra, the Cupertino tech giant also introduced the new Apple Watch SE at its Far Out event last year. The Apple Watch SE (2022) replaces the original Apple Watch SE with an updated chip, a slightly redesigned casing, and more. It also starts at a lower price now compared to the original Apple Watch SE (2020), making it a solid option for those who are looking to buy a cheaper Apple Watch to pair with their new iPhones.

Related
Apple Watch Series 9 vs Watch SE 2: Which smartwatch should you get?
Should you pick up an all-new Series 9, or should you save some cash and grab an SE 2?

Apple didn't launch a new SE Watch model alongside the Apple Watch Series 9, but the regular Apple Watch SE from 2022 is still worth considering if you are already stretching your budget trying to buy a watch to go with your iPhone. It debuted along the Apple Watch Series 8 model and the Apple Watch Ultra, and it packs the S8 chip which is still pretty reliable. As far as the features are concerned, the Apple Watch SE (2022) supports heart rate monitoring and the ability to track all the standard sets of workouts. However, you'll be missing out on ECG, SpO2, and even the new temperature sensor, all of which are supported by the mainline Apple Watch models.

In terms of design, the new Apple Watch SE (2022) looks very similar to a lot of other modern Apple Watches on the market. It has thicker bezels compared to the new Series 9, but you can mask it rather easily by using a darker watch face. The bottom case of the Apple Watch SE (2022) is made of nylon composite materials that are color-matched to the bezels. The overall performance of the Apple Watch SE should be significantly better than its predecessor, and it works very well for the most part. The new Apple Watch SE has also been updated to the latest watchOS version, and you won't miss out on any essentials like the crash detection feature.

Garmin Bounce
Best for kids

A reliable watch with text and voice messaging

The Garmin Bounce introduces kids to health insights and gamifies moving around to help promote healthy habits. Parents can also locate their kids through the watch and reach them via text which kids and read and respond right on the screen.

Pros
  • Supports two-way text and voice messaging
  • Safety controls including location tracking and emergency contact support
  • Parental controls with contact list management support
Cons
  • Requires LTE subscription exclusive to Garmin
  • Does not support phone calls
$150 at Garmin

If you're not ready to buy a full-fledged smartwatch for your kids yet, then consider checking out the Garmin Bounce. It's a kid-friendly device offering good connectivity, parental controls, and a host of other essentials. It also comes with a bunch of preloaded apps and games that your kids will find useful for everyday use. Additionally, this particular watch will also reward your kids with more features and fun activities in the Garmin Jr. app as they move and clock more activity in the real world while wearing the watch.

Related
Garmin Bounce kids smartwatch review: Will make both kids and parents happy
The Garmin Bounce isn't a flashy smartwatch for kids, but it performs as advertised, making is great option for families.

The only thing to note about this particular smartwatch is that you'll need to subscribe to a proprietary Garmin subscription plan to unlock its LTE connectivity. It's essential to access features like text and voice messages, so keep that in mind. Parents can also take advantage of the parental control features to access their ward's real-time location with notifications and enable the class mode to ensure they're not distracted by the watch during schoolwork.

The Garmin Bounce is a simple-looking watch that's lightweight and feels comfortable to wear every day. It sports a 1.3-inch LCD touchscreen and a handful of buttons on the sides to help you navigate through the watch. It also offers excellent battery life, so you don't have to worry about charging it every day or every time your kid is about to step out of the house.

There's no shortage of reliable smartwatches on the market in 2024

Out of all the options mentioned in this particular collection, the Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic and the Apple Watch Series 9 are two of the best smartwatches you can buy right now. The Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic has a beautiful circular design that resembles a timeless analog timepiece, but with all the advanced tech you’d expect in a flagship smartwatch, such as ECG, 90-plus activities and a 2,000 nit bright display. The Apple Series 9 also has a bright 2,000 nit display, and many of the same features you’ll find on the Ultra 2, including the blazing fast S9 chip, but for a smaller price tag.

The Galaxy Watch 6 Classic beautifully blends the Watch 4 and the Watch 5, bringing back the physical turning bezel that makes navigating the UI a breeze. The aforementioned battery life is an improvement from previous generations as well as the Exynos W930 processor, which makes navigating through your watch a consistently smooth endeavor.

  • Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic
    Best smartwatch for Android users

    The Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic can take over 90 different activities and make sure your heart is in check with heartbeat and rhythm notifications.

    $394 at Amazon$340 at Samsung
  • Apple Watch Series 9
    Best smartwatch for Apple users
    $329 $399 Save $70

    Powered by the blazing fast S9 chip, you not only get a faster and brighter watch, but also Siri that doesn't require internet connection.

    $329 at Amazon$349 at Best Buy$399 at Apple

The Apple Watch Series 9, on the other hand, is a solid option for iPhone users. It's not as kitted as the Ultra model, but the Series 9 has the same S9 chip, and the ever-so-useful Double Tap gesture feature for hands-free control of your watch and phone. On-board Siri is also a boon and makes it easy to manage tasks like timers and reminders without the need for internet connection.

This is an ever-evolving list, so we'll continue to keep an eye on the market to see if we can add new options to this post.
item.content:encoded:
A smartwatch is an extension of your smartphone. You get all the benefits of the best fitness trackers as well as access to your personal life, such as text messages, emails, calendars, and notifications. But when it comes to the best smartwatches, the choices are nothing short of abundant. Apple alone has several generations, all of which you can score discounts through ever-changing Apple watch deals.

But don’t let that abundance of choice scare you. This article has a great selection of the best smartwatches that will fit your needs. Even if you aren’t team Apple or Samsung, there are plenty of other options from top-tier tech brands like Garmin, Google, and Amazfit to meet your smartwatch needs.

  • Source: Samsung
    Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic
    Best smartwatch for Android users
    $340 at Samsung
  • Source: Apple
    Apple Watch Series 9
    Best for Apple users
    $329 at Amazon
  • Source: Google
    Google Pixel Watch 2
    Great safety features
    $350 at Amazon
  • Source: Garmin
    Garmin Instinct 2 Solar
    Best for battery life
    $400 at Garmin
  • Fitbit Sense 2
    Great for health enthusiasts
    $247 at Amazon
  • OnePlus Watch 2
    Best Wear OS watch for battery life
    $300 at OnePlus
  • Source: Withings
    Withings ScanWatch 2
    Best hybrid smartwatch
    $350 at Best Buy
  • Source: Amazfit
    Amazfit T-Rex 2
    Best rugged smartwatch for most users
    $200 at Amazon
  • Source: Apple
    Apple Watch Ultra 2
    Premium Apple Watch
    $738 at Amazon
  • Source: Garmin
    Garmin Venu 3
    Best health-data insights
    $424 at Amazon
  • Source: Amazfit
    Amazfit GTR Mini
    Best budget smartwatch
    $120 at Amazon
  • Source: Apple
    Apple Watch SE 2
    Best budget smartwatch for iPhone users
    $240 at Amazon
  • Source: Garmin
    Garmin Bounce
    Best for kids
    $150 at Garmin

Our favorite smartwatches in 2024

Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic
Best smartwatch for Android users

A smartwatch packed with great heart sensor capabilities and great battery life.

The Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic can take over 90 different activities and make sure your heart is in check with heartbeat and rhythm notifications.

Pros
  • Automatic activity tracking
  • Good battery life
  • Bright display
Cons
  • Wristband is uncomfortable
  • Some health features require Samsung phone pairing
$394 at Amazon$340 at Samsung$400 at Best Buy

The watchmakers at Samsung listened to your feedback to bring what you liked from the Samsung Galaxy Watch 4 while advancing the tech from the Samsung Galaxy Watch 5. Making a comeback is the rotating bezel that XDA's former Editor, Chris Wedel, raved over in his review of the Galaxy Watch 6 Classic. Not only does the rotating bezel make for a simpler user experience, as it conveniently navigates through menus and scrolls through messages, but it also provides that oh-so-sweet click as you turn it.

Related
Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic review: An excellent generational mashup
The Galaxy Watch 6 Classic brings back a smartwatch favorite along with a spec bump. It's a great experience, even if it's too familiar

Aside from the bezel, the Watch 6 Classic sports a Super AMOLED display with Sapphire Crystal glass for added protection from scratches and scrapes. Throw in a brighter 2,000-nit display from previous versions, and not only is this one of the best-looking displays in the Samsung Smartwatch family, but it is also the brightest, helping you see the display even in direct sunlight. The battery found notable improvements as well from previous versions. You can expect about 40 hours of battery life, which smashes the likes of the Apple Series 8 Watch, which is only rated for 16 hours.

Another big advancement from previous versions is the ability to detect heart rhythm irregularities. The advanced sensors inside of the Watch 6 Classic will send you notifications if it detects an irregular heartbeat, helping you stay safe and healthy. Of course, all of the usual suspects are available as well, including heart rate, step counter, workout duration, and distance. But the Samsung Smartwatch can even recognize when you’ve begun certain activities, like swimming, even if you forgot to manually track it. This helps you leave no workout unaccounted for. If you’re looking for a new Android Watch, The Samsung Galaxy Watch 6 Classic is a stellar choice.

Apple Watch Series 9
Best for Apple users

The best Apple Watch for most users

$329 $399 Save $70

The design of the Apple Watch stayed the same from previous iterations, but the features improved. The Double Tap gesture provides great functionality without needing to touch the display. And a new processor and brighter display makes the watch faster and easier to view in bad lighting conditions.

Pros
  • On-device Siri
  • Double Tap gesture
  • Bright display
Cons
  • Might not be worth upgrading if you have Series 7 or Series 8 Apple Watch
$329 at Amazon$349 at Best Buy

The “squircle” design remains the same compared to previous generations, but the 2,000-nit display helps you see the watch face better in harsh lighting conditions, such as direct sunlight. Along with a brighter display comes a new S9 processor. With the faster, more improved processor comes along on-device Siri. With it, you can ask Siri to do tasks like set an alarm, or create a reminder, without the need for an internet connection. It makes managing your watch, and your daily tasks, much easier.

Related
Apple Watch Series 9 review: More of the same, but it'll be enough
The Apple Watch Series 9 doesn't look that exciting, but the upgrades it packs make it one of the best smartwatches — again.

Much like the design, the battery life stayed relatively the same as well. You can expect about a full day's worth (24 hours) of battery life before it needs a recharge. Even though the design aesthetically looks the same, Apple is making huge strides environmentally by going completely carbon-neutral with this watch, crafting the watch itself, as well as its packaging, all from recycled materials.

Activity tracking remains elite. Although not new, Apple Watch Series 9 can detect when you’ve begun exercising, and track it, even if you forget to do it manually. It also has an accurate step counter and all the great heart rate monitoring and irregular heartbeat rhythm notification functionality that we’ve become accustomed to over past generations of Apple Watch.

Google Pixel Watch 2
Great safety features

The redo of the Pixel Watch is a winner

The Pixel Watch 2 makes the necessary improvements from the first generation. Improved all-day battery life and health tracking sensors help boost the overall quality of the watch. On-board health coaches can help you keep a specific pace while running, and also guide you through meditation sessions. It’s a great watch to manage your day- to-day.

Pros
  • All-day battery life
  • Improved health tracking
  • Great safety features
Cons
  • Only available in one size
$350 at Amazon$350 at Best Buy$350 at Google Store

Making improvements where it sorely needed it, the Pixel Watch 2 is a much better representation of a smartwatch that actually belongs in the conversation with the best smartwatches available. The battery life lasts all day, even with the great-looking 1.2-inch AMOLED display set to always-on.

Related
Google Pixel Watch 2 review: Second time's the charm
The Google Pixel Watch 2 fixes the glaring flaws of the first watch and keeps what worked. The price is still perhaps too high, though

One of the strengths of the Pixel Watch 2 is the synergy between smartwatch and phone. You can receive and respond to texts from the watch. It is compatible with WhatsApp, arguably the most widely used messaging app outside the U.S. And unsurprisingly, yet super helpful, apps like Gmail and Google Calendar also work as if you were on your phone or computer. All the more reason to keep your phone in your pocket.

A new heart-rate sensor is highly accurate, and the step counter does a great job to accurately count steps, and ignore fake one’s, like when you’re moving your arms around while you’re talking. A new electrodermal activity sensor also detects changes in your temperature to detect elevated levels of stress. When this happens you’ll receive notifications on how to reduce stress with suggested breathing and mindfulness exercises.

Garmin Instinct 2 Solar
Best for battery life

Military-grade protection and solar charging

The Garmin Instinct 2 Solar is perfect for those who spend most of their time outdoors. The smartwatch has an impressive 28-day max battery life and solar charging capabilities for less downtime. It also tracks a plethora of outdoor activities, everything from mountain to sea.

Pros
  • Incredible battery life solar charging
  • Rugged form-factor
  • Comes in two different sizes
Cons
  • Expensive price tag
  • No storage for music
$400 at Garmin$410 at Amazon$400 at Best Buy

The Garmin Instinct 2 Solar comes with the promise of "never-ending battery life" for active users who spend some time outdoors every day. This particular watch charges using a transparent "power-glass" covering the screen and the panels on the surrounding edges. This glass is capable of keeping the watch topped up for essentially unlimited battery life, provided you spend at least three hours every day in direct sunlight. The goal is to expose the watch to 50,000 lux of light every day, so you don't have to plug it into a charger for more juice.

Related
Best fitness trackers in 2024
From simple trackers to more sophisticated options that double up as a smartwatch, these are the best fitness trackers to buy in 2023.

The Garmin Instinct 2 Solar lacks a touchscreen, but it more than makes up for it with its intuitive button-based interface. It supports all the smart features that you'd expect from a typical smartwatch in 2022, including notification pop-ups, GPS, workout tracking, heart rate tracking, and more. The Instinct 2 Solar tracks a vast number of activities, including running, walking, cycling, swimming, strength training, cardio, and more. It syncs with the Garmin Connect app on your smartphone to give you additional insights too.

The Garmin Instinct 2 Solar comes in a bunch of different models, but the standard version with solar charging starts at $450. This particular smartwatch is not for everyone, and certainly not for those who spend a lot of time indoors. But if you're an active outdoor person who spends long enough each day in the sun, then you may never have to plug it in for charging.

Fitbit Sense 2
Great for health enthusiasts

A fitness tracker at heart, but still a decent smartwatch.

$247 $300 Save $53

With a slim profile and impressive battery life that can last up to six days, the Fitbit Sense 2 is a great companion for exercising and as an everyday wearable. It features automatic workout detection to help you keep track of your workouts and is available in several colors.

Pros
  • Lightweight
  • Great battery life
  • Google Maps and Google Wallet available
Cons
  • No music controls
$247 at Amazon$250 at Best Buy

The Fitbit Sense 2 has a lightweight, smooth and easy to wear profile that is built for all day comfort. That’s great considering there's a good chance you won’t need to take it off to charge for several days. The maximum achievable battery life is six days, but our Editor, Chris Wedel mostly experienced three days of battery life in his review of Sense 2. You can also get a full day’s worth of battery life just by charging your watch for 15 minutes. Long battery life and quick charging are both impressive and handy.

Related
Fitbit Sense 2 review: A fantastic wearable that straddles the line between two worlds
The Fitbit Sense 2 is walking a very fine line between being a smartwatch and a fitness tracker — great at one but not so much at the other.

It’s a bit odd that Google owns Fitbit, but Google Assistant isn’t available. Instead, you have access to Amazon Alexa on the watch for your hands-free needs. And when it comes to fitness data, it’s robust to say the least, but some of it is behind a subscription. For instance, if you want sleep data, such as Sleep Profiles, which provides insights on your sleep patterns, you’ll have to pay for Fitbit Premium which is $10 a month or discounted at $80 for the year. All of your health data automatically syncs with the Fitbit app, where you can go in-depth on your health and fitness tracking data.

And if you enjoy sharing your fitness accomplishments with a community of like-minded fellow exercisers, Fitbit has done a great job of creating a community to do so. You can share your fitness progress, and even compete with fellow enthusiasts through the smartwatch and Fitbit app.

OnePlus Watch 2
Best Wear OS watch for battery life

Multiday battery on a single charge

The OnePlus Watch 2 features a 1.43-inch OLED display and runs on Google's WearOS 4 for native support of Google services. It's also powered by two chips, including Qualcomm's Snapdragon W5, to handle energy efficiency and performance, giving it superior battery life to rival wearables from Google and Samsung. 

Pros
  • You don't need to bring a charger for a weekend trip
  • Great design and build quality
  • Reliable and snappy performance
Cons
  • On-screen keyboard difficult to type, and no option to scribble text
  • The crown is loose and can be twisted, but doesn't actually do anything
$300 at OnePlus

The OnePlus Watch 2 is the latest Wear OS smartwatch to land on the market, but this one is slightly different from the others that are out there. That's because the OnePlus Watch 2 promises to last for up to 100 hours on a single charge, which is a pretty big claim for a Wear OS smartwatch. My colleague Ben was able to able to test the battery life of this watch, and he says you can use it for up to four days with controlled, light usage. That makes it one of the best Wear OS watches out there for battery life.

Related
OnePlus Watch 2 review: The rare WearOS watch with good battery life
The OnePlus Watch 2 fixes the biggest issue of the first watch — by running WearOS, it finally has access to Google services that most rely on

The TicWatch Pro 5 that we reviewed last year offers similar battery power, but it also costs slightly more than the OnePlus Watch 2. The OnePlus Watch 2 can also achieve those battery life figures without relying on the secondary low-power display. It instead employs a relatively low-powered chipset called BES 2700 for basic tasks like tracking steps and keeping alarms. More intense and resource-intensive Wear-OS related tasks are handled by the Snapdragon W5 chip, which is known to be pretty reliable for everyday use.

As far as the other features are concerned, the OnePlus Watch 2 sports a 1.43-inch OLED screen with a resolution of 466x466, and up to 1,000 nits brightness. It also ships with Wear OS 4 out of the box, so it comes with all the bells and whistles you'd expect to see on a modern Wear OS watch. The OnePlus Watch 2 also has a heart rate monitor, and it also comes with features like workout detection and other fitness-centric features.

Withings ScanWatch 2
Best hybrid smartwatch

Offers the best of both worlds
